concerns.text: Task T018 ('Implement null hypothesis testing... via label permutation') claims to satisfy SC-001 but omits the explicit generation of the empirical null distribution via label shuffling. SC-001 requires measuring against a null distribution generated by 'shuffling essentiality labels [deferred] times'. T018 describes comparing against a distribution 'generated by T019' (graph rewiring), which is a different null model (FR-010). This task silently relaxes the SC-001 requirement by conflating the label-permutation null with the graph-rewiring null, failing to deliver the specific verification step mandated by the spec.

Task T019 ('Implement graph rewiring...') is scoped only to 'generate a set of degree-preserving random graphs'. It does not include the step of computing the correlation coefficient on these randomized graphs to form the null distribution required by FR-010 ('compare the observed correlation coefficient against the null distribution'). Without computing the correlation on the rewired graphs, the task cannot satisfy the 'compare' clause of FR-010, effectively weakening the requirement to a partial implementation.

Task T040 ('Refactor code/main.py to accept a list of confidence thresholds') is scheduled in Phase 6, after T030 ('Refactor code/main.py to loop over thresholds'). T030's description implies the refactor is already complete ('Refactor... to loop over thresholds'). This creates a logical contradiction where the feature (sensitivity analysis loop) is implemented in T030 before the structural refactor (T040) that enables it is defined. This violates the constraint that tasks must not introduce retroactive narrowing or logical impossibilities; the implementation of FR-007 (Sensitivity Analysis) is structurally broken by this ordering.

Task T009 states: 'if fetch fails... skip the comparative test (PGLS) gracefully (FR-009)'. However, FR-009 in the spec explicitly mandates skipping PGLS only if 'effective sample size (n) < 10'. The condition for skipping due to a missing phylogenetic tree is covered in the 'Assumptions' section of the spec, not FR-009. By mapping the tree-fetch failure to FR-009, the task misrepresents the requirement, potentially leading to incorrect error handling logic that conflates sample-size power issues with data-availability issues.
